attributes.title: Brute Force — Investigate
attributes.visState:
{"title": "Brute Force — Investigate", "type": "markdown", "aggs": [], "params": {"fontSize": 12, "openLinksInNewTab": true, "markdown": "### 🔓 Brute Force → Successful Logon — investigate
**Open:** [Discover (raw alerts)](/app/discover) · [Wazuh app](/app/wazuh) · [MITRE T1110](https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1110/) · [T1078](https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1078/)

Logons that **succeeded after repeated failures** (rule 40112) — treat every targeted account as a **possible credential compromise**.

**Who / what / where:** `data.srcip` = the **attacker** · `data.dstuser` = the **account** · `agent.name` + `agent.ip` = the **target host and its IP** (auth/SSH logs carry no separate destination IP, so the agent *is* the target) · `decoder.name` = the **service** (sshd, …).

💡 Make hosts clickable: add a URL field-formatter to `agent.id` (see the recipe README)."}}

attributes.title: Brute Force → Successful Logon
attributes.description: Successful brute-force logons (rule 40112): attacker IPs, targeted accounts, TARGET host+IP and service, with a full attacker->account->target detail table.
